6.
General
6.1.Qualification Unit
Vendor must qualify individually each equipment, manufacturing method and material. 
• PCB/FPC vendor should use one type of machine  per process per project APN per layer. If not feasible, vendor must consult 
1
Apple Fabrication team, and receive written approval from Fabrication team DRI before use in an actual Apple build or MP. 
Qualification steps include at the minimum UPD 1, 2, CQRA and 3x3 LQ lots. Refer to scenario 3 in qualification scenario table.
• PCB/FPC vendor is responsible to control materials and raw materials throughout project NPI until its end of life, and must ensure 
no process changes at material vendor sites. Any changes at material level is considered to create a “new material”. Vendor must 
follow new material workflow for qualification.
6.2.Risk Assessment and qualification scenarios
A. Equipments and methods: Refer to following pages for details
B. Material qualification: vendor must publish proposal for Evaluation contents (methods, quantity, criteria). Apple DRI must review 
and approve before proceeding.
6.3.Qualification Procedure
Refer to following pages for details.
Every steps must be done before MP.
6.4.Test Specimen
• Test specimen for each test item are detailed “Product Quality and Capability Confirmation” and “CQRA” sections for each process.
• When test coupons are required, test coupon design and materials must be the same as actual product and have same layer count 
in stack-up for via integrity evaluation. In any other cases, vendor must use actual product. Design of the actual product to use is 
determined by design compatibility. Refer to procedure pages for compatible design definition.
6.5.Equipments, Chemicals List
From P1 and at every build before input, vendor must publish a list of qualified equipments, chemicals and material to Apple DRI, 
including DFM, SQE and GSM.
Vendor must update the list at every build, reflecting all changes, additions, and removals from previous builds.
Vendor must publish the final list before MP. 
 A type of machine is a combination of all physical characteristics including but not limited to equipment’s vendor, model, design, 
1
internal components’s types, dimensions, mechanical construction, etc. (e.g. bath dimensions, chemical used, nozzles locations or 
directions, etc.) as well as software, and electrical settings. If any characteristic is different, then it's considered as different type.

Definitions and notes: 
*1 Unit Process Development (UPD)
• Before qualification, each individual line or machine must have UPD, once 
per machine and per compatible design.
• Every time a machine is relocated, it needs to have UPD again even if the 
relocation is within the same factory (e.g. floor 1 to floor 4)
• UPD has 2 steps:
• Step 1: build small quantity of DoE parts to set nominal process 
parameters and compare the product quality. It should not be statistically 
significantly worse than qualified machines.
• Step 2: Build again small quantity of DoE parts at process parameter 
corners based on nominal settings from step 1. Quality must be within 
specifications.
• Items to confirm at each step include items detailed in “Product Quality and 
Capability Confirmation” table on each process section, not CQRA.
• UPD parts must go through POR intent process flow. Vendor shall not skip 
process steps.
• Machines must be fully loaded as MP, when vendor does UPD. Actual 
products or dummy panels can be used to replicate full loading. Including 
all chemical or wet process that use immersion (no need for the ones that 
use only spray), and following mechanical or dry process: Plasma dry 
desmear, Hot press laminator, Cover layer attach+pressing, Stiffener/EMI 
shielding attach+baking, Soldermask Pre-curing, Soldermask Post thermal 
curing, UV curing, Soldermask Plasma cleaning.
*2 Exceptions for UPD step 2
The following processes need only UPD step 1: AOI, AVI, electrical test, 
inkjet legend printing, outline cut/singulation using punch, coverlay cut using 
punch, and panel slitting using punch.
*3 Compatible design: A design with any of the following features: 1) layer 
count equal or higher, 2) line/space equal or smaller, 3) pad size equal or 
smaller, 4) laser/NC drill via size equal or smaller, 5) aspect ratio equal or 
higher, 6) geometry tolerance equal or tighter.
Not compatible design: A design with any of the following features. 
Equipments impacted by these features need to run through UPD.
• different dielectric or copper materials or with different properties (e.g. 
thickness, PI color, resin content, etc.) or manufacturing sites (including 
raw materials), or different model name, including suffix and line up 
number (e.g.ABC-1234_2 and ABC-1234-T, etc.)
• different solder mask or coverlay materials, 
• different surface finish.
*4 Equipment exactly the same as qualified equipment
This includes all physical characteristics including but not limited to 
equipment’s vendor, model, design, internal components’s types, 
dimensions, mechanical construction, etc. (e.g. bath dimensions, chemical 
used, nozzles locations or directions, etc.) as well as software, and electrical 
settings. If any characteristic is different, then it's considered as different 
type.

Not compatible design: A design with any of the following features. 
Equipments impacted by these features need to run through UPD.
• different dielectric or copper materials or with different properties (e.g. 
thickness, PI color, resin content, etc.) or manufacturing sites (including 
raw materials), or different model name, including suffix and line up 
number (e.g.ABC-1234_2 and ABC-1234-T, etc.)
• different solder mask or coverlay materials, 
• different surface finish.
*4 Equipment exactly the same as qualified equipment
This includes all physical characteristics including but not limited to 
equipment’s vendor, model, design, internal components’s types, 
dimensions, mechanical construction, etc. (e.g. bath dimensions, chemical 
used, nozzles locations or directions, etc.) as well as software, and electrical 
settings. If any characteristic is different, then it's considered as different 
type.
*5 Scope of any changes in recipe, process parameters, or 
manufacturing methods: includes but not limited to: panel layout or size, 
glass mask. 
Any changes at material level is considered to be “new material”. Vendor 
much follow new material workflow for qualification. This includes but is not 
limited to: changes of material vendor, material vendor process/line/
equipment/factory, raw material vendor process/line/equipment/factory
For bonding sheet, coverlay punching die: any change from soft tool to 
hard tool after P2, and any change of cavity quantity per tooling after EVT.
Exceptions: below features are not taken into account for risks assessment:
• Copper pattern, via count, via location and outline design change in Apple 
ODB++.
• Electrical testing jig / fixture design change in Apple ODB++.
• DES etching or flash etching line speed adjustment to meet trace width 
nominal.
• ENIG nickel and gold parameter adjustment to meet thickness nominal.
• Inspection machines with MSA (Measurement System Analysis) confirmed 
and showing no difference with POR machines statistically.
• Tightened process parameters window.

• After CQRA is finished and if quality of these lots is confirmed acceptable 
and process is qualified for the build as a result, then these lots can be 
used and shipped as POR parts, with NPI Ok2ship and full CQRA 
validated and approved.

• Filter rate = quantity judged by AI correctly / suspected NG quantity judged by AOI or AVI 
• Escape rate = defect quantity escaped from AI / overall AI inspection quantity
• Po is the relative observed agreement among raters, and Pe is the hypothetical probability of chance agreement

8.6.1.AFVI, VRS, artificial intelligence
8.6.1.1.Product Quality and Capability confirmation
Definitions:
• Filter rate = quantity judged by AI correctly / suspected NG quantity judged by AOI or AVI 
• Escape rate = defect quantity escaped from AI / overall AI inspection quantity
• Po is the relative observed agreement among raters, and Pe is the hypothetical probability of chance agreement
